records.ety: [OD. wijssegger or G. weissager a foreteller, prophet, from weissagen to foretell, to prophesy, OHG. wīssag<?/n, corrupted (as if compounded of the words for wise and say) fr. wīzzag<?/n, fr. wīzzag<?/ a prophet, akin to AS. wītiga, wītga, from the root of E. wit. See Wit, v.]

quotes: Pythagoras learned much . . . becoming a mighty wiseacre.

defs: One who makes undue pretensions to wisdom; a would-be-wise person; hence, in contempt, a simpleton; a dunce.
